hook.headline: GPT-5 Pro + Codex Cloud = your coding agent now runs without your laptop.
hook.sub: delegate · close laptop · come back to a PR

cards.label: 01 · what
cards.title: Codex Cloud spins up a VM for every task.
cards.body: You say "fix this race condition" or "add these tests". Codex Cloud clones the repo into a sandboxed VM, runs the work, and sends back a PR. Your machine never has to be on.

cards.label: 02 · why
cards.title: You can run 5 tasks at once.
cards.body: Local agents are bound to one terminal, one model, one process. Cloud agents stack — kick off 5 bugs in 5 different VMs, go to lunch, review the PRs that came back.

cards.label: 03 · model
cards.title: GPT-5 Pro is the default brain.
cards.body: Codex Cloud runs against GPT-5 Pro by default — strongest OpenAI model for code reasoning. Multi-file edits, test generation, refactors. Pairs with Codex CLI for local dev.

cards.label: 04 · cost
cards.title: $20 to try. $200 to live in it.
cards.body: ChatGPT Plus ($20/mo) gets you limited Codex Cloud tasks. ChatGPT Pro ($200/mo) gets you generous quota + GPT-5 Pro included. Teams ($25/seat) for shared pools.

cards.label: 05 · vs
cards.title: vs Claude Code / Cursor agent.
cards.body: Claude Code + Cursor agent run locally — fast iteration, you watch every step. Codex Cloud runs remotely — slower per task, but you can run 5 in parallel and walk away. Use both.

take.use_if: Your bottleneck is reviewing PRs, not writing code — Codex Cloud parallelises the writing.
take.skip_if: You're a tight-loop interactive coder — local agents (Cursor / Claude Code) give faster feedback per edit.
